immutable_core.semantic_intent: source-recorded urinalysis nitrite ordinal result event
immutable_core.semantic_definition: An event-level urinalysis nitrite result retaining a normalized binary ordinal result domain with raw source result provenance and best-available encounter/stay linkage.
immutable_core.semantic_grain: one retained source-recorded urinalysis nitrite result event
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_nitrite_urinalysis_result must preserve source-recorded urinalysis nitrite result semantics and must not collapse into numeric urine chemistry, microbiology culture evidence, urinary tract infection phenotype, or diagnosis code truth.

ordinal_result_contract.target_entity_grain: laboratory result event with best-available admission and ICU-stay linkage
ordinal_result_contract.result_domain_rule: retain only normalized `negative` and `positive` nitrite result states
ordinal_result_contract.raw_result_retention_rule: retain raw value, itemid, label, specimen, unit, and abnormal flag provenance for audit
ordinal_result_contract.normalization_rule: map approved negative text variants to negative and approved positive text variants to positive; exclude blank placeholders rather than treating them as negative
ordinal_result_contract.no_row_interpretation: no retained interpretable nitrite result under the approved source rule; this does not prove nitrite negativity or absence of urinary infection

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_nitrite_urinalysis_result outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.
